coupling.summary: Intra-package dependencies clean. Missing Platform abstraction bridge — dialogs use window.api directly, coupling app components to Electron types and breaking web support.

debuggability.summary: All failure scenarios collapse into 1-2 generic i18n keys with no description propagation. IPC null cancellation can crash (JSON.parse(null)). IPC handlers lack try/catch. No Effect.withSpan annotations.

convergence.summary: Follows established patterns well. BLOCKER: importSession depends on InstanceState.context (via Session.use.create()) but import has no active project instance. Orphaned exportSession() function. Misleading sanitization scope.

surface_area.summary: ~23 files across 4 packages. Plan references non-existent routes/session.ts (actual: groups/session.ts + handlers/session.ts). i18n touches 18 locale files. SDK bypass means raw fetch() in desktop.

testability.summary: Core schema (fix-A) 4/5. Server (fix-B) 3/5. Desktop IPC (fix-C) 1/5 — zero IPC test infra. Dialogs (fix-D/E) 1/5 — zero component test infra. Commands (fix-F) 2/5. i18n (fix-G) 5/5.

error_clarity.summary: No typed error for Schema decode failures (cryptic ParseError → 500). IPC crashes unhandled. Null cancellation unguarded. 500-msg limit unenforced. Missing Effect.withSpan.

reversibility.summary: Core + UI groups not independently revertible. IPC bridge (3 files) HIGH conflict risk. i18n (18 files) VERY HIGH coordination surface. Untyped IPC bridge creates silent runtime failures if reverted out of order.

blocking_findings.description: importSession calls Session.use.create() which requires InstanceState.context (projectID, directory, worktree). During import from file, there is no active project instance. The plan must specify how session creation works without InstanceState.

Plan says dialogs call window.api.* directly from app components, bypassing the Platform type abstraction. Must add exportSessionData?()/importSessionFile?() as optional methods on Platform type (following exportDebugLogs precedent) and implement in desktop renderer createPlatform().

Fix-B references non-existent file routes/session.ts. Actual server routes are groups/session.ts (HttpApiGroup) and handlers/session.ts (handlers). The POST /session/import endpoint must be added to both.

No typed error handling for Schema.decode(ExportedSession)(body) failures. Effect Schema ParseError is cryptic. Must wrap with catchTag('ParseError', ...) → InvalidRequestError(400) following existing catchTag patterns.

IPC handlers for session-export-data and session-import-file use writeFileSync/readFileSync without try/catch. Disk full (ENOSPC), permission (EACCES), or deleted-file (ENOENT) errors crash the main process.
No null guard on IPC cancellation. Import dialog: await window.api.importSessionFile() returns null on cancel. JSON.parse(null) throws SyntaxError. Export dialog: null path passed to success toast.

conditions: Resolve InstanceState.context dependency for importSession — accept explicit projectID/directory parameters or use createNext directly
Add exportSessionData?() / importSessionFile?() as optional methods on Platform type (platform.tsx), implement in desktop renderer createPlatform()
Correct file references from routes/session.ts to groups/session.ts + handlers/session.ts
Add typed error for Schema decode: InvalidRequestError(400) with catchTag('ParseError', ...)
Add try/catch to IPC handlers around writeFileSync/readFileSync
Add null guards in both dialog handlers for IPC cancellation (if result === null return)
Clarify sanitize toggle label — 'Remove permission rules, share URL, and local paths only' with info note that message content is not sanitized
Either add call site for exportSession() or remove it to avoid dead code

subagent_summaries.coupling-auditor: 10 dependency edges examined. Edge 7 (Platform bridge missing) is only blocking coupling issue. Edge 3 (exportSession orphaned) is informational. All other edges acceptable.
subagent_summaries.surface-area-mapper: 23 files across 4 packages. Plan references non-existent routes/session.ts. Platform type needs new optional methods. SDK bypass means desktop uses raw fetch(). 15 non-English locales silently fall back.
subagent_summaries.isolation-tester: Core schema 4/5 testable (pure functions). Desktop IPC 1/5 (zero test infra). Dialogs 1/5 (zero component test infra). i18n 5/5 (trivially testable).
subagent_summaries.error-trace-auditor: 7 failure scenarios traced. Most critical: Schema decode errors invisible to user, IPC crashes unhandled, null cancellation causes SyntaxError, no span annotations, misleading sanitization scope.
subagent_summaries.convergence-checker: Pattern consistency generally strong. REAL BLOCKER: InstanceState dependency for importSession. Sanitization scope misleading. Creates tracked migration debt on SyncEvent write path.
subagent_summaries.debuggability-forecaster: 1/5 rating — all failures collapse to 1-2 generic i18n keys with no description. Worst: large session silent truncation, migration errors with opaque refs, no telemetry for export/import.
subagent_summaries.reversibility-checker: Core+UI groups not independently revertible. IPC bridge (3 files) HIGH conflict risk. i18n (18 files) VERY HIGH coordination surface. Untyped IPC bridge creates silent failure modes.

follow_up_issues: Message-content sanitization (v2) — API key scanning, path stripping in tool outputs
Desktop IPC test infrastructure — mock dialog.showSaveDialog/showOpenDialog
SyncEvent vs EventV2 migration debt — re-examine import path when Postgres migration finalizes
SDK regeneration alignment — migrate import from raw fetch to generated client after v1
500-message limit enforcement — add count protection in both export/import paths
Add semantic error discrimination in dialog catch handlers (network vs 400 vs 500)
